key_insights.insight: The happiness we experience when we attain a desired object is actually the freedom from wanting itself, but we quickly get caught in a cycle of wanting again.
key_insights.quote: But what we're looking for is the absence of wanting.That's the happiness we achieve when we get what we want, is a kind of freedom from wanting.

key_insights.insight: We are constantly bombarded with messages telling us we are lacking, which significantly affects our stress and confidence.
key_insights.quote: We're constantly made to feel when something's missing,something's always missing, we're not good enough.

key_insights.insight: The pursuit of something provides meaning, but it's a dopamine-driven cycle that is more exciting than the attainment, leaving us empty.
key_insights.quote: So we're always in pursuit of something. And the chemistry of our body in the state of pursuitis that chemical dopamine.The interesting thing about dopamine is it falls awayjust before you get what you want.

key_insights.insight: Meditation is not about clearing your mind, but about changing your relationship with your thoughts.
key_insights.quote: It's about learning how to be less controlledby your mind, but it's not about getting rid of the thoughts.In fact, the thoughts are quite helpful.

key_insights.insight: The definition of meditation is waking up inside your thoughts and then gently returning to your breath, building mental strength each time you return.
key_insights.quote: That is the meditation, waking up inside your thoughts.That is the definition of meditation.

key_insights.insight: The point of meditation is not just to become calmer, but to be present, precise, focused, and less controlled by distractions and negative thoughts.
key_insights.quote: It's about being present. It's about being less controlled by distraction, be less controlled by negative thinking. And if you can do that,you can achieve more.

key_insights.insight: You are not your past, and clinging less to it leads to freedom and less suffering.
key_insights.quote: But Buddhism brings in a whole fresh perspective, whichis that you are not your past.

key_insights.insight: True forgiveness isn't about condoning, but about freeing yourself from toxic rage and hurt.
key_insights.quote: But I think forgiveness is a strength or a power and it's actually nothing to do with the other person.You're not going to necessarily write them a letter and say,I've forgiven you, but you're freeing yourself.

actionable_advice.advice: Practice microscopic moments of meditation throughout the day.
actionable_advice.how_to: Become aware of yourself in busy situations by focusing on the ground under your feet, dropping your shoulders, or being aware of your breathing.

actionable_advice.advice: When experiencing strong emotions, drop the story and look at the feeling.
actionable_advice.how_to: Focus on the sensation in your body (e.g., a twisting knife, a sinking feeling) without judgment or analysis; relate to the sensation with kindness.

actionable_advice.advice: Start a daily meditation practice with 10 minutes of focused breathing.
actionable_advice.how_to: Sit upright (on a chair if needed), set the intention of compassion, become aware of your hands, shoulders, and then the rising and falling of your chest or belly; gently return to the breath when your mind wanders, knowing that this brings strength. Meditate first thing in the morning, giving up the need to check phone first.

actionable_advice.advice: Change your relationship with your thoughts
actionable_advice.how_to: Focus on the breath. When the mind wanders, recognize that you are lost in your thoughts and gently bring yourself back to the breath. Each time you do this you choose where to send your mind.

memorable_stories: Yarlong's experience of climbing over the wall during a four-year retreat due to intense depression and anxiety.
Tara scooping up Yarlong, sick at age 21, and taking him to the monastery.
Yarlong describes finding out that his teacher was murdered by a monk and using meditation to send love into the flames.
Yarlong's description of an abusive band leader when he was a teenager.
contrarian_claims: Chucking away paraphenalia is better than keeping it for spiritual practice.
We should send love to feelings we hate.
Meditation can be good, even when you hate it.
The purpose of meditation is not clearing the mind.
It is better to forgive the feeling then the individual.
Buddhism doesn't try to attract followers so it grows.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Buddhist Teacher: No One Is Talking About This Hidden Epidemic! The Western Lie Behind Depression and Anxiety
